address: 200A Baldwin St (I Love Churros, corner Augusta/Baldwin)

windows_detail.note: Fully covered by storefront wrap-around. Large plate glass windows on both Baldwin and Augusta sides. Grey-painted ground floor cladding with 'I LOVE CHURROS' lettering in white/red on windows.
Two windows visible on Baldwin-facing facade, white frames set in exposed brown/red brick. Satellite dish mounted between them.

doors.note: Corner entrance at Augusta/Baldwin intersection, recessed under storefront canopy

storefront.note: Wrap-around storefront on corner lot. Ground floor entirely clad in dark grey panels. Red fixed canopy/awning runs across full Baldwin frontage. Signage both illuminated on upper facade and painted on windows.

condition_notes: Ground floor recently clad in modern grey panels. Upper brick in good condition, original. Satellite dish on facade. Corner building in active commercial use. Some graffiti on adjacent walls but the building itself is well-maintained.

address: 191 Baldwin St (Rumble Boxing)

windows_detail.note: Three tall rectangular fixed panes forming storefront. Dark green/black metal frames. Interior visible with pink/red lighting.

doors.note: Glass door integrated into storefront glazing, center position

storefront.note: Single-storey commercial unit. Entire facade is modern black horizontal metal siding above storefront. Neon 'Rumble' sign in red cursive script. Low colourful mural/artwork strip runs along base below windows. Outdoor cafe seating with small tables and chairs.

condition_notes: Recently renovated modern commercial facade. Black horizontal metal siding in excellent condition. Clean, contemporary fit-out.

address: 195 Baldwin St (Flamingo Vintage)

windows_detail.type: storefront plate glass with diamond security grilles
windows_detail.note: Two large storefront windows with white frames and diamond-pattern expandable security grilles (half-closed). Heavy sticker/graffiti coverage on lower portions and surrounding walls.
Two windows visible in painted white/grey brick upper storey. Dark frames. Left window partially obscured.

doors.note: White-framed glass commercial door between two storefront windows

storefront.note: Large yellow banner sign reading 'FLAMINGO VINTAGE' with flamingo logo spans full width above storefront. Ground level heavily covered in stickers and graffiti. Also has 'FLAMINGO VINTAGE' text painted on left side wall/adjacent facade.

condition_notes: Upper facade painted white/light grey brick is weathered and stained. Extensive graffiti/sticker coverage at ground level and on side walls. Security grilles suggest vandalism concern. Overall structure intact but cosmetically worn. Yellow signage banner in reasonable condition.

address: 195 Baldwin St (Flamingo Vintage, side angle)
note: Side/oblique angle of same building as PXL_20260318_222309452.jpg. Provides additional detail on the upper storey and left party wall.

windows_detail.note: Same as frontal view. Grilles fully visible from this angle.
Clearer view of upper windows. Both have dark brown frames. Left window has 'FLAMINGO VINTAGE' signage painted across second-floor wall between and below windows.

condition_notes: Side angle confirms heavy sticker/graffiti coverage. Upper painted brick is peeling in places. Flat roof edge visible with minor deterioration. Neighbouring black-clad Rumble building visible to right.

address: 173 Baldwin St (Vida Natural and Organic Products)

windows_detail.note: Large single storefront window on left side. Products visible inside. Black painted brick below window, dark cladding.
Two evenly spaced windows in brown/red brick upper storey. White frames. Soldier course brick lintels above each window.

doors.note: Glass entry door with black frame, center of storefront

storefront.note: Bold black-and-white striped awning spans full storefront width. 'Vida' signage on awning in script. Security accordion grille on left window (retracted). Products displayed in window.

condition_notes: Upper brick in good condition, original mortar intact. Ground floor recently fitted out with black cladding and striped awning. Clean and well-maintained commercial unit. Fire hydrant at sidewalk.

address: 185 Baldwin St (Jimmy's Coffee)

windows_detail.note: Entire ground floor is storefront with security scissor grille (closed). Large mural of the Joker (Batman villain) painted on left side wall. Dark painted cladding.
Two windows in brown brick upper facade. White frames. Stone or concrete flat lintels. Small green metal railing/balcony visible at roofline above with potted plants.

doors.note: Behind closed scissor grille. Dark frame entry door.

storefront.note: Dark olive/green fabric awning with gold 'JIMMY'S COFFEE' lettering. Scissor security grille fully closed across storefront. Joker mural on left party wall. Purple/green graffiti art extends to left side.

condition_notes: Upper brick facade in solid condition. Green railing/balcony at roof level with vegetation. Mural on party wall well-executed. Grille suggests nighttime security concern. Bicycle parked out front. Overall well-maintained.

address: 189 Baldwin St (green-trimmed building)

windows_detail.note: Storefront fully behind security scissor grille (closed). Decorative green tile/panel cladding with stepped zigzag pattern in green and cream/white tiles mid-facade. Two art murals flanking the storefront (left: colourful fish scene, right: skull/face artwork).

Two windows in stucco/painted upper storey. Dark green frames matching trim. Upper wall is beige/cream stucco. Decorative moulding between storefront and upper floor in Art Deco-inspired geometric pattern.

doors.note: Behind scissor grille, dark green frame

awning.type: fixed flat awning with orange/red stripe
awning.colour: dark green with orange accent stripe

storefront.note: Distinctive Art Deco-influenced facade. Green tile/panel cladding in stepped zigzag pattern. Narrow fixed awning above storefront with orange/red stripe. Green pilasters frame storefront. Murals on both flanking panels. Rooftop metal railing/balcony visible with plants.

condition_notes: Upper stucco somewhat weathered. Green tile cladding intact but dated. Art Deco moulding detail survives. Roof railing with plants. Murals on flanking panels in reasonable condition. Security grille fully closed.

address: 187 Baldwin St (Sea Kings Fish Market)

windows_detail.note: Full storefront with large signage panel. Blue-painted lower bulkhead with 'SEA KINGS' and 'FISH MARKET' lettering. Glass entry door visible.
Upper facade covered by large 'SEA KINGS 416 593 9948' signage panel in gold/tan with red 3D lettering. Windows may exist behind signage but not visible.
Small window or decorative opening in the prominent front-facing gable. Dark brown frame. Gable has metal balcony railing at base.

doors.note: Glass entry door on right side of storefront, dark frame

storefront.note: Full-width storefront. Blue bulkhead below. Green-and-white striped awning. Large gold/tan signage panel with red 3D 'SEA KINGS' letters and phone number. Blue wave motif painted on bulkhead.

party_wall_right_material: shared wall (green pixel art mural visible on right neighbour)
condition_notes: Bay-and-gable form with prominent front gable preserved. Entire lower facade clad in commercial signage obscuring original fabric. Gable has ornamental detail surviving (bargeboard, possible shingle decoration). Blue paint on bulkhead is bright but commercial. Balcony railing at gable base. Adjacent building has green pixel-art mural.

address: 181 Baldwin St (Golden Patty)

windows_detail.note: Large single storefront window spanning most of ground floor width. Food products and sun logo ('Golden Patty') visible inside. Colourful graffiti mural on left side (bulkhead area). Parking meter in front.
Three evenly spaced windows in buff/tan brick upper storey. White frames. Clean brick with no decoration.

doors.note: Glass door with white frame on right side of storefront

storefront.note: Yellow signage band with red 'GOLDEN PATTY' lettering and phone number spans full width above storefront. Sun logo on window. Colourful graffiti mural on left-side bulkhead. Clean, functional Caribbean food shop.

condition_notes: Buff brick upper storey in good condition, clean and unaltered. Storefront well-maintained, signage bright. Some graffiti on adjacent surfaces but building itself is clean. Utility pole partially obscures right side view.

address: 179 Baldwin St (graffiti garage/roses)

windows_detail.note: No conventional windows. Entire facade consists of two garage/roll-up doors covered in elaborate graffiti murals. Left door: colourful cartoon character (monkey with pizza). Right door: large rose flowers (pink/red/blue) with 'MEKOHAURE 7s' text. Blue-green painted metal lintel/header above doors.

doors.note: Left garage door with cartoon mural
Right garage door with rose mural

storefront.note: Single-storey garage/utility structure. Two roll-up metal doors fully covered in graffiti murals. Blue-green painted lintel/beam across top. No signage. Appears to be a non-retail service/storage unit.

party_wall_left_material: stucco/render (colourful mural continues from left neighbour)
condition_notes: Simple single-storey garage structure. Metal doors in functional condition. Extensive graffiti murals (artistic quality, appears intentional/sanctioned). Blue-green painted header beam showing some wear. Ice/icicles visible on header suggesting water drainage issue.

address: 185 Baldwin St area (Woofdawg Hotdog)
note: This appears to be a ground-floor storefront view, possibly the ground floor of 183 or 185 Baldwin. The upper storey is stucco/painted with one small window visible at upper left. Mural wraps around from 179 Baldwin.

windows_detail.note: Single large storefront window on left side. Green-and-white checkered tile/paint surround. Interior lit showing hot dog service counter. Colourful cartoon mural covers rest of ground floor (lumberjack character with hot dogs, psychedelic food imagery).
One small window visible at upper left of beige/cream stucco upper wall

doors.note: Glass entry door at far left of storefront, white frame

storefront.note: Full-width storefront with 'WOOFDAWG HOTDOG' text in red letters on black band above storefront. Colourful cartoon mural wraps entire ground floor facade (connects to 179 Baldwin mural). Green/white checkered tiles around window. Wooden bench outside.

condition_notes: Upper stucco is plain and somewhat weathered. Ground floor entirely covered in mural art, which is vibrant and well-maintained. Simple functional structure. Some peeling at stucco/wall junction.

address: 189 Baldwin St area (Laya Skye Jewelry)

windows_detail.note: Single large storefront window, frosted/opaque glass. Black-painted brick below window and surrounding frame. Very minimal storefront.
One window visible in red/brown brick upper storey. Black frame.

doors.note: Recessed entry on far left side, dark door, appears to be separate entrance (possibly residential upper unit)
Shop entry door, minimal black frame

storefront.note: Black signage board above storefront reads 'LAYA SKYE JEWELRY' in silver/white letters. Small dark red/maroon awning below signage. Storefront is restrained and minimal — black painted lower facade, frosted glass window. Pink building visible to left (neighbour), green/white checkered to right.

condition_notes: Upper brick in good condition, original red/brown. Ground floor recently renovated with clean black paint and minimal modern aesthetic. Narrow building, well-maintained.

address: 202 Baldwin St (Kensington Vape)
note: Photo is rotated 90 degrees (landscape orientation captured as portrait). Building is on the north side of Baldwin St.

windows_detail.note: Grid of approximately 6 storefront panes (2 columns x 3 rows) with white frames. Vape product signage and imagery in windows (pink/red vape graphics). Numbers '202' and '19' visible. Dark brick below and around storefront.
One wide horizontal window in tan/gold stucco upper wall. Black frame. Upper wall is parging/stucco in gold/tan colour. Decorative rosette medallion visible on upper wall.

doors.note: Dark red-framed glass entry door with 'KENSINGTON VAPE' signage above

storefront.note: Small corrugated metal canopy above entry. Storefront windows filled with vape product graphics. Red 'KENSINGTON VAPE' signage. Adjacent to another business on right with food signage (donuts/sandwiches). Weathered wooden fence/hoarding at far right side. Bare tree in front.

party_wall_right_material: brick with wooden fence/hoarding at grade
condition_notes: Upper stucco/parging in tan/gold is somewhat weathered but has decorative rosette medallion (heritage detail). Lower brick darkened with age. Storefront windows completely covered in advertising graphics. Wooden hoarding/fence at right suggests adjacent vacant lot or construction. Graffiti on lower walls.

address: 376-378 Spadina Ave (B&J Trading, Baldwin side view 1)
note: Photo is rotated 90 degrees counterclockwise. Large 3-storey red brick commercial building on Spadina Ave viewed from Baldwin St side. B&J Trading signage visible.

windows_detail.note: Four tall narrow storefront windows with red-painted metal frames. Dark tinted glass. Some appear to be display windows for B&J Trading. Graffiti/chalk drawings on lower walls.
Five windows on the Baldwin St (side) elevation. White frames. Flat stone/concrete lintels and sills. Some windows appear to have interior blinds.
Five windows matching second floor pattern. White frames. Security bars on some ground/second floor windows.

doors.note: Main entrance appears to be on Spadina Ave side. Red-framed commercial entry visible.

storefront.note: Large oval/elliptical 'B&J TRADING' sign with blue background and white/gold lettering. '352 SPADINA AVE' address visible. Ground floor has tall storefront windows in red frames. Security bars on some windows.

condition_notes: Red brick is original and in fair condition. Some mortar deterioration visible. Ground floor has chalk/graffiti marks. White efflorescence/staining on some brick areas. Large signage somewhat dated. Security bars suggest area concerns. Building is structurally sound but cosmetically aged.

address: 376-378 Spadina Ave (B&J Trading, second angle)
note: Photo rotated 90 degrees. Same building as previous, slightly different angle showing more of the Baldwin St elevation. Nearly identical view. B&J Trading sign and red brick clearly visible.
condition_notes: Same as first angle. Red brick with some mortar deterioration. Graffiti/chalk on lower facade. Structurally sound.

address: 376-378 Spadina Ave (B&J Trading, third angle - closer)
note: Photo rotated 90 degrees. Closer view of the same building showing ground floor detail and the Baldwin/Spadina corner. More detail visible on storefront windows and graffiti. Security grille visible on ground floor windows.

windows_detail.type: storefront plate glass with security bars
windows_detail.note: Closer view reveals security bars/grilles on some ground floor windows. Red-painted steel frames. Chalk/white graffiti tag visible on brick between windows.
Three windows visible from this closer angle (partial view of elevation)

doors.note: Ground floor entry with grille visible, red frame

condition_notes: Closer view confirms graffiti tags in white chalk/paint on lower brick. Security grilles on windows. Brick mortar joints showing wear. Pizza shop signage visible at very far right edge (adjacent ground floor unit).

address: 376-378 Spadina Ave (B&J Trading, fourth angle - Spadina frontage)
note: Photo rotated 90 degrees. This view shows more of the Spadina Ave frontage including the ground floor commercial units. Satellite dish visible on facade. Stepped parapet/setback visible at upper floors. Pizza shop and other commercial tenants at ground level.

windows_detail.type: storefront plate glass with security grilles
windows_detail.note: Multiple storefront units along Spadina frontage. Some windows have security grilles/bars. 'PIZZA' signage visible on right corner unit. Mixed commercial tenancies.
Windows on Spadina elevation. Satellite dish mounted between second and third floor windows.
Upper floor windows. Building steps back slightly at upper portion on left side.

doors.note: Multiple entry doors for different commercial units along Spadina frontage

condition_notes: Wider view confirms this is a substantial 3-storey corner commercial building. Satellite dish on facade. Multiple ground floor tenants. Brick is aged but structurally sound. Some staining and graffiti. Pizza shop unit on corner has bright signage. Adjacent concrete block wall of neighbouring building visible.

address: Baldwin St / Spadina Ave (vacant lot / construction hoarding)
note: Wide view looking east along Baldwin from Spadina intersection. Shows a long hoarding/construction fence covered in posters and advertisements along the south side of Baldwin. Behind the hoarding, rooflines of buildings further along Baldwin are visible. Bare trees. Yellow fire hydrant visible. This is a vacant/development site, not a building facade.

storefront.signage_text: Various advertising posters (Miu Miu, beer ads, 'Born Ready for the Home Opener')

condition_notes: Vacant lot or active development site. Long plywood/metal hoarding covered in advertising posters and wheat-pasted ads. Not a building -- no facade to reconstruct. Provides context for the Baldwin/Spadina intersection. Red brick building (376-378 Spadina) visible at right edge.

address: Baldwin St / Spadina Ave (vacant lot hoarding, second angle)
note: Same vacant lot/hoarding as previous photo but from a slightly different angle looking more westward. Hoarding continues with advertising posters. Street lamp and utility pole visible. Buildings along Baldwin visible in background behind bare trees. Same non-building site.
condition_notes: Same vacant lot hoarding as PXL_20260318_224133826.jpg from different angle. Not a building.

address: 229 Augusta Ave (graffiti shutter detail, Augusta Coffee Bar area)
note: Close-up nighttime shot of a security roller shutter covered in a large graffiti mural depicting two stylized faces/figures (one wearing an orange cap, one larger brown figure). 'KENSINGTON' text visible at top. This is a detail of the ground-floor security shutters of 229 Augusta Ave. Not a full building facade shot.

doors.note: Large metal roller shutter fully closed, covered in mural art depicting two characters

party_wall_left_material: painted (tropical plant mural visible at left edge)
condition_notes: Nighttime close-up of roller shutter mural. High-quality street art. Metal shutter in functional condition. Graffiti tags at base. This is a detail shot only, not a full facade.

address: 229 Augusta Ave (Augusta Coffee Bar, full facade)
note: Nighttime shot showing the full ground-floor facade of Augusta Coffee Bar at 229 Augusta Ave, corner of Baldwin. Wide single-storey commercial storefront with multiple roller shutters covered in colourful murals. 'AUGUSTA COFFEE' text visible in white on upper wall band. Bike rings on sidewalk.
facade_material: painted stucco/render (upper band), metal roller shutters (ground)

windows_detail.note: No conventional windows visible. Entire ground floor consists of 3-4 roller shutter bays covered in elaborate graffiti murals (dragon/monster, tropical plants/flowers, abstract, and the two-figure portrait). All shutters closed (nighttime). 'AUGUSTA COFFEE' text on white band above shutters. Blue graffiti tag ('clouds/bubble letters') visible on upper parapet.

doors.note: Entry doors behind closed roller shutters, not visible

storefront.note: Wide single-storey commercial building with multiple shutter bays. White text 'AUGUSTA COFFEE' on upper facade band. 3-4 mural-covered roller shutters spanning full width. Corner location at Augusta/Baldwin. Blue graffiti on upper parapet. 'EAR' text visible at right edge suggesting adjacent business.

condition_notes: Nighttime view. Building is a simple single-storey commercial structure. White upper facade band is clean. Roller shutters functional. Graffiti murals appear to be sanctioned street art (high quality). Blue graffiti tag on upper parapet is likely unsanctioned. Bike rings along sidewalk. Building reads as a low-rise market-type unit.

address: Augusta Ave / Baldwin St intersection (night, snowy)
note: Nighttime snowy street scene at the Augusta/Baldwin intersection. Shows the corner building with graffiti murals on roller shutters (229 Augusta, same as previous). 'GREELZ' neon sign visible across the street on a separate building. Street-level context shot showing intersection, utility poles, snow on ground. Gable rooflines of Baldwin St buildings visible in background.
condition_notes: Contextual nighttime/snowy intersection shot. Not a single building facade. Useful for understanding the intersection geometry, streetscape, and building massing visible in background. 229 Augusta corner building visible at left with mural shutters. GREELZ restaurant across street. Gable rooflines of Baldwin buildings visible in distance.

address: Augusta Ave / Baldwin St (night, view from patio)
note: Nighttime shot taken from under a patio/awning structure looking across the Augusta/Baldwin intersection. GREELZ restaurant neon sign visible across the street. Steam/mist rising from street. 'Longboards' text visible on right-side building glass. Mostly a contextual street scene, not a facade analysis shot.
condition_notes: Contextual nighttime shot from under patio. Steam/mist visible. GREELZ restaurant across intersection has neon signage. Longboards shop visible at right. Not useful for individual building facade reconstruction.

address: Augusta Ave / Baldwin St (night, looking north along Augusta)
note: Nighttime shot rotated 90 degrees, looking north along Augusta Ave from Baldwin intersection. Yellow-painted lattice/porch structure visible in foreground (patio of a restaurant). White car parked. Buildings along Augusta visible in background with lit storefronts. Snow on ground. Mostly contextual -- provides streetscape depth and building massing along Augusta north of Baldwin.
condition_notes: Nighttime contextual street view looking north along Augusta Ave. Yellow lattice patio structure in foreground. Snow on sidewalk. Provides streetscape context and massing information but not a single-building facade shot. Lit storefronts along Augusta visible in background.
